caveats: Demo uses a synthetic SaaS revenue mart.
The brief does not infer metrics outside documented dbt context.

charts.filters: All documented rows in demo fixture
charts.title: MRR by segment

charts.filters: All documented rows in demo fixture
charts.title: Monthly MRR growth

caveats: Fixture uses documented recurring revenue only and excludes one-time services.
findings.claim: Total MRR increased from $816K to $821K in March, but monthly growth dropped from +$53K to +$5K.

caveats: Segment-level attribution is based on the documented model grain: one row per month and customer segment.
findings.claim: Enterprise MRR fell by $17K in March, while mid-market and SMB added $22K combined.

queries.sql:
select
  month,
  segment,
  mrr
from mrr_by_month
order by month, segment

with totals as (
  select
    month,
    sum(mrr) as total_mrr
  from mrr_by_month
  group by 1
),
changes as (
  select
    month,
    total_mrr,
    total_mrr - lag(total_mrr) over (order by month) as mrr_change
  from totals
)
select *
from changes
order by month

question: Why did net revenue growth slow in March?
summary: MRR still grew in March, but the growth rate slowed from +$53K in February to +$5K in March because enterprise contraction offset continued SMB and mid-market expansion.
